sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: fat is 15g. omega 3 is doing serious work here. for a tuber, this mostly lives or dies on carb behaviour.
sections.subtitleText: fat is 15g. omega 3 is doing serious work here. for a tuber, this mostly lives or dies on carb behaviour.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: carbs is 41g. the fibre support does a lot of the heavy lifting. for tubers, the carb side decides whether the food feels stable or flimsy.
sections.subtitleText: carbs is 41g. the fibre support does a lot of the heavy lifting. for tubers, the carb side decides whether the food feels stable or flimsy.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: protein is 3.4g. protein quality is clearly one of the selling points. protein is usually limited here, so the other sections have to carry more.
sections.subtitleText: protein is 3.4g. protein quality is clearly one of the selling points. protein is usually limited here, so the other sections have to carry more.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: vitamin a at 0% DV, vitamin c at 6% DV stand out most. that is not enough to move the verdict very far on its own.
sections.subtitleText: vitamin a at 0% DV, vitamin c at 6% DV stand out most. that is not enough to move the verdict very far on its own.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: potassium at 13% DV, magnesium at 7% DV, iron at 4% DV stand out most. that stays fairly limited overall.
sections.subtitleText: potassium at 13% DV, magnesium at 7% DV, iron at 4% DV stand out most. that stays fairly limited overall.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: pros first. strong comfort-food appeal because a very real reason people like them, even if the nutrition fit is weak. still retains some tuber identity because not nutritionally empty, just much worse than stronger tuber forms. satisfying texture and taste because part of the real-world appeal, especially for content framing.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: now the downsides. fried format is a major category negative because this drags tubers away from the cleaner energy-food role the category is supposed to reward. glycemic behaviour is rough because a major practical strike against the food in a carb-behaviour category. energy density is much harsher than ideal because much easier to overshoot calories than with cleaner tuber forms.

closing.summary: Fries stands out for strong comfort-food appeal and still retains some tuber identity. but it also gets held back by fried format is a major category negative and glycemic behaviour is rough. Best only when you want easy carbs and can accept the missing upside.

closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?
